arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou ‘not in the happiness business’ over Maddison’s playing time
arts.sum: Spurs manager credits midfielder’s ‘enormous self-belief’ Maddison scored twice in rout of Manchester City Ange Postecoglou said that everybody would love to be as self-assured as James Maddison, not least some of his other players at Tottenham, as he reflected on the vice-captain’s importance to the collective. It felt as though Maddison had a point to prove at Manchester City last weekend when he returned to the XI after two Premier League matches as a substitute and he proved it, scoring the first two goals in the 4-0 win. arts.ttl: Postecoglou holds transfer meeting with Johan Lange and has dressing room chat with Tottenham star
arts.sum: I would much rather we’re exciting to watch and maybe it is edge of the seat stuff and sometimes we don’t get the reward, like we could have tonight, but no, I think if you want those kind of games, there are plenty in world football you can watch every weekend. On Wednesday night, the Australian was asked whether the draw against Roma meant the priority of the next three Europa League games is to simply win ahead of artistic merit and make sure Tottenham finish in the top eight in the big table to bypass the play-off round.
